text: 'Owner-only' is not the same as 'owner/local-only'; the draft conflates them. Owner credential does not enforce locality.

Option A (extract computeMaintenanceItems from hot memory_orient path) is under-de-risked; a byte snapshot cannot catch latency, access-control, or detail-mode regressions. Prefer Option B with a parity test against memory_orient(detail:full).
Per-section graceful degradation is required, not optional: one failing sub-query must not 500 the whole health payload. Shape: total auth gate, then top-level ok + per-section ok + sanitized errors.

Embedding-queue design (raw status counts + time-based stuck) cannot diagnose the motivating incident (16 stale-model entries). Needs model-relative counts (generated_current/stale/null, reembedding_backlog), worker telemetry, and no time-based stuck claim without a real claim timestamp.

Metadata leakage (namespace/classification counts) is real; owner-only is the right boundary only if truly owner/local. Add defense-in-depth: new aggregate helpers take ctx/owner scope, not just a handler gate.
'access-denied count (audit_log)' is unsupported by the code. Replace with measurable cross_zone_blocks and redaction_events, or add real denial telemetry first.
Retrieval metrics should use bounded aggregate helpers, not an unbounded O(N) iteration of getInsightsByEntry across all entries.
Draft's {error:'access_denied'} denial semantics do not match the repo's local non-owner idiom (owner-only fields nulled). Mirror memory_status's non-owner path.
Payload contract/versioning missing. Add schema_version + generated_at; treat sections as add-only stable contracts since Heimdall couples to them.
Section error strings must be sanitized like consolidation last_error (redact API-key-shaped content; no raw exception text, token prefixes, or credential paths).

Heimdall auth identity resolved against live memory: deployed Heimdall uses an owner-scoped MCP client at 127.0.0.1:3030, so an owner-only tool returns it data — provided its credential resolves to principalType 'owner' (a service/agent principal would get nothing).
